figures.caption: Table 5.2: Performance Comparison on CSI300 and CSI500 Datasets, with the best value bold and the suboptimal value underlined. Improvement (%) is relative to the suboptimal baseline.
Table A.2: Subgroup analysis by industry sector on CSI500. Red values indicate the best model per sector. (Companion to Figures A.1 and A.2.)
Table A.3: Subgroup analysis by geographic region on CSI500. Red values indicate the best model per region.(Companion to Figure A.1 and A.3.)

Figure A.2: Absolute IC and Rank IC comparison by industry sector on CSI500. ACT (red) dominates in relationally dense sectors (Electronics, Pharma, Food & Bev.), while iTransformer is competitive in some sectors (Mining, Materials). Companion to the relative-improvement heatmap in Figure A.1a.

Figure A.3: Absolute IC and Rank IC comparison by geographic region on CSI500. Dense stock clusters (NE China, North China, South China) show clear ACT dominance; NW China is the sole exception. Companion to Figure A.1b.
Table 5.4: Portfolio backtesting on CSI500 (TopKDropout, K=50, N=5). All metrics except CR are computed on excess returns (without cost), with the best value bold and the 2nd/3rd value underlined.
Figure 5.1: Cumulative Return on CSI500 from 2023 to 2025. ACT (red) substantially outperforms all baselines.

Figure 4.1: Overall architecture of the ACT framework. The input is decomposed into trend, fluctuation, and shock components via TCD. The trend component is processed by the Progressive Structural Purification Encoder (PSPE), while fluctuation and shock are routed to dedicated isolation branches (FCI and SCI). All representations are recombined by Adaptive Component Fusion (ACF) for final forecasting.

Figure A.1: Relative improvement (%) of ACT over the best performance among two baselines (XGBoost, iTransformer), by industry sector and geographic region on CSI500. Darker green indicates larger gains; red indicates the baseline outperforms ACT.
